seoTitle: Granville vs Grassy Head — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Granville vs Grassy Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Granville and Grassy Head.
heading: Granville vs Grassy Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Granville, New South Wales and Grassy Head, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Grassy Head has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Granville (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Granville or Grassy Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Granville has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Grassy Head scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Granville scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Grassy Head scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Granville or Grassy Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Grassy Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
